Officials of the Great Northern
who have been handling the Japa-
nese labor on that line believe that
the Japanese government has is
sued a call to it's subjects In the
United States to return to Japan
and join the army. Within the past
few weeks nearly 200 Japanese la.
borers have quit the Great North.
ern in this state alone, and every
Japanese asked why he would quit
has replied, me go home to join
army"


Offers of increased pay have
been disdained by these Japs and
a bonus offered by the railroad dis
regarded. Today there is not JAP
working along the Coast Line be-
tween Seattle and Vancouver, B
c., where formerly 200 were EN-
ployed. The exodus has also ex'
tended over the Cascade division OF
the main line, and officers of the
road say it resembles the sudden
departure of the hundreds of ia
panese laborers for the native land
at the opening of the Russian war
